contract.name: v2/federal_accounts/account_number.md
contract.description: Returns summary fields and child Treasury Account aggregates for a single federal account (account_number) and fiscal_year.

account_number.description: Federal account symbol (Agency Identifier + Main Account Code), e.g., '011-1022'. Used to select which federal account record to return.
account_number.constraints: Required. Observed route-compatible format is `^\d{3}-\d{4}$` (e.g., `011-1022`). If the value does not match this format, the server responds `404 Not Found` with an HTML body. If it matches the format but no federal account exists, the server responds `400` with JSON `{ "detail": "Cannot find Federal Account..." }`.

fiscal_year.description: Appropriations fiscal year for which totals and children Treasury Accounts are returned.
fiscal_year.constraints: Optional. If omitted, the API defaults to its current FY (observed default was `2026` at probe time). If provided, it must be parseable as an integer; leading zeros (e.g., `02022`) and trailing whitespace (e.g., `2022 `) were accepted. Non-integer values (e.g., `2022.0`, `abcd`, empty string) can trigger a `500 Server Error` with an HTML response body. Duplicate `fiscal_year` query params are accepted but yield a single-year response with unclear selection; avoid duplicates for determinism.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year context for returned totals/children. If `fiscal_year` is omitted, this is a number (API default FY). If `fiscal_year` is provided, this is a string echo of the raw query value (may include leading zeros, whitespace, or sign) and may not be a normalized year representation.

id.description: Internal database identifier for the federal account.

agency_identifier.description: 3-digit agency identifier component of the federal account symbol.

main_account_code.description: Main account code component of the federal account symbol.

bureau_name.description: Bureau name associated with the federal account.

total_obligated_amount.description: Sum of child Treasury Account `obligated_amount` values; `null` when there are no child Treasury Accounts for the selected fiscal year.

total_gross_outlay_amount.description: Sum of child Treasury Account `gross_outlay_amount` values; `null` when there are no child Treasury Accounts for the selected fiscal year.

total_budgetary_resources.description: Sum of child Treasury Account `budgetary_resources_amount` values; `null` when there are no child Treasury Accounts for the selected fiscal year.

children.description: List of Treasury Accounts applicable to the federal account and fiscal year. Empty when there are no child Treasury Accounts for the selected year.

body.detail: Cannot find Federal Account with the code of '000-0000'

quirks: Both `/api/v2/federal_accounts/{account_number}` and `/api/v2/federal_accounts/{account_number}/` returned `200` for valid account numbers (trailing slash is optional).

If `fiscal_year` is omitted, `response.fiscal_year` is a number (API default FY). If `fiscal_year` is provided, `response.fiscal_year` echoes the raw query string (e.g., `fiscal_year=02022` returns FY2022 data but `response.fiscal_year` is `"02022"`).

`fiscal_year` accepts integer-like strings such as `2022`, `02022`, `2022 `, `0`, and `-1`. Non-integer values (e.g., `2022.0`, `abcd`, empty string) can produce a `500` with an HTML response body (not JSON).
Supplying `fiscal_year` multiple times returns `200` but yields a single-year response; observed year selection was inconsistent (avoid duplicates).
For years with no child Treasury Accounts, `children` is `[]` and the `total_*` fields are `null`.
risks: Clients should always send a canonical `fiscal_year` (e.g., `2022`), because the API echoes the raw query string in `response.fiscal_year` and accepts non-canonical integer strings (leading zeros, trailing whitespace, sign) that can confuse caching, logging, or downstream parsing.
Non-integer `fiscal_year` values can trigger `500 Server Error` with HTML content rather than a `4xx` JSON validation response; callers should validate `fiscal_year` client-side.
Error surface is inconsistent: `400`/`405` return JSON with a `detail` field, while `404` (bad `account_number` format) and `500` (bad `fiscal_year`) return HTML.
Supplying duplicate `fiscal_year` query params yields a non-obvious single-year response (avoid duplicates for determinism).
The default `fiscal_year` changes over time; specify `fiscal_year` explicitly for repeatability.
gaps: Many invalid `fiscal_year` inputs produce a generic `500` HTML error page rather than a JSON validation error; a structured validation error schema is not observed for these cases.

The API echoes the raw `fiscal_year` query value in `response.fiscal_year`; the normalization/parsing used for data lookup is only inferable from probe comparisons (e.g., `02022` returning the same totals as `2022`).

mismatches: Docs narrative describes a landing page listing all federal accounts, but the documented path requires {account_number} and probes show it returns a single account object.
Docs describe response.fiscal_year as a number, but probes show response.fiscal_year is a number when defaulted and a string (echo of query param) when a fiscal_year query param is provided.
gaps: Many invalid `fiscal_year` inputs produce a generic `500` HTML error page rather than a JSON validation error; a structured validation error schema is not observed for these cases.

The API echoes the raw `fiscal_year` query value in `response.fiscal_year`; the normalization/parsing used for data lookup is only inferable from probe comparisons (e.g., `02022` returning the same totals as `2022`).
risks: Clients should always send a canonical `fiscal_year` (e.g., `2022`), because the API echoes the raw query string in `response.fiscal_year` and accepts non-canonical integer strings (leading zeros, trailing whitespace, sign) that can confuse caching, logging, or downstream parsing.
Non-integer `fiscal_year` values can trigger `500 Server Error` with HTML content rather than a `4xx` JSON validation response; callers should validate `fiscal_year` client-side.
Error surface is inconsistent: `400`/`405` return JSON with a `detail` field, while `404` (bad `account_number` format) and `500` (bad `fiscal_year`) return HTML.
Supplying duplicate `fiscal_year` query params yields a non-obvious single-year response (avoid duplicates for determinism).
The default `fiscal_year` changes over time; specify `fiscal_year` explicitly for repeatability.
